content:
Hi,

I'm trying to cross-compile the Linux kernel with Rust support from macOS (because why not?) but rust/kernel/lib.rs compilation fails with the following error:

 INFO rustc_metadata::locator dylib reading metadata from: /Volumes/Build/linux-build-llvm/rust/libmacros.so
 INFO rustc_metadata::locator Rejecting via proc macro: expected false got true
 INFO rustc_metadata::locator metadata mismatch
error[E0463]: can't find crate for `macros`
  --> /Volumes/Build/linux/rust/kernel/lib.rs:75:9
   |
75 | pub use macros;
   |         ^^^^^^ can't find crate*

So libmacros.so is a proc_macro crate but the file is somehow rejected when compiling the kernel lib crate ? I understand that proc_macro crates are compiled for the host and it seems to be the case.

I'm looking for hints in order to understand if the problem comes from the proc_macro crate file or from the cross-compilation context. Do you know where I can look next ?

Thanks.

target.json

{
"arch": "aarch64",
"data-layout": "e-m:e-i8:8:32-i16:16:32-i64:64-i128:128-n32:64-S128",
"disable-redzone": true,
"features": "+strict-align,+neon,+fp-armv8",
"llvm-target": "aarch64-linux-gnu",
"max-atomic-width": 128,
"target-pointer-width": "64",
"emit-debug-gdb-scripts": false,
"frame-pointer": "may-omit",
"stack-probes": {"kind": "none"}
}

rustc command line for libmacros and libkernel**

RUSTC P rust/libmacros.so

rustc --edition=2021 -Zbinary_dep_depinfo=y -Dunsafe_op_in_unsafe_fn -Drust_2018_idioms -Dunreachable_pub -Dnon_ascii_idents -Wmissing_docs -Drustdoc::missing_crate_level_docs -Dclippy::correctness -Dclippy::style -Dclippy::suspicious -Dclippy::complexity -Dclippy::perf -Dclippy::let_unit_value -Dclippy::mut_mut -Dclippy::needless_bitwise_bool -Dclippy::needless_continue -Wclippy::dbg_macro --emit=dep-info=rust/.libmacros.so.d --emit=link=rust/libmacros.so --extern proc_macro --crate-type proc-macro --crate-name macros /Volumes/Build/linux/rust/macros/lib.rs

RUSTC L rust/kernel.o

OBJTREE=/Volumes/Build/linux-build-llvm rustc --edition=2021 -Zbinary_dep_depinfo=y -Dunsafe_op_in_unsafe_fn -Drust_2018_idioms -Dunreachable_pub -Dnon_ascii_idents -Wmissing_docs -Drustdoc::missing_crate_level_docs -Dclippy::correctness -Dclippy::style -Dclippy::suspicious -Dclippy::complexity -Dclippy::perf -Dclippy::let_unit_value -Dclippy::mut_mut -Dclippy::needless_bitwise_bool -Dclippy::needless_continue -Wclippy::dbg_macro --target=./scripts/target.json -Cpanic=abort -Cembed-bitcode=n -Clto=n -Cforce-unwind-tables=n -Ccodegen-units=1 -Csymbol-mangling-version=v0 -Crelocation-model=static -Zfunction-sections=n -Dclippy::float_arithmetic -C target-feature="-neon,-fp-armv8" -Z branch-protection=bti,pac-ret,leaf -Copt-level=2 -Cdebug-assertions=n -Coverflow-checks=y -Cforce-frame-pointers=y @./include/generated/rustc_cfg --extern alloc --extern build_error --extern macros --extern bindings --extern uapi --emit=dep-info=rust/.kernel.o.d --emit=obj=rust/kernel.o --emit=metadata=rust/libkernel.rmeta --crate-type rlib -L./rust --crate-name kernel /Volumes/Build/linux/rust/kernel/lib.rs

Running target/debug/rmetadump /Volumes/Build/linux-build-llvm/rust/libmacros.so

crate metadata dump excerpt

python3 dump-rust-metadata.py /Volumes/Build/linux-build-llvm/rust/libmacros.so
compressed data size = 10792
decompressed data size = 27062
rusth�#rustc 1.67.1 (d5a82bbd2 2023-02-07)�versions�vtable�pin_data�
pinned_drop�derive_zeroable��������%�<( Crate for all kernel procedural macros.�%+ Declares a kernel module.���E The type argument should be a type which implements the [Module]��H6 trait. Also accepts various forms of kernel metadata.��9�P C header: include/linux/moduleparam.h��S�( [Module]: ../kernel/trait.Module.html��+�

use kernel::prelude::*;��� module!{��
:
:

Version info

  • linux 6.3.0
  • rustc 1.67.1 (d5a82bbd2 2023-02-07)
  • host : Darwin imac-alexis 20.6.0 Darwin Kernel Version 20.6.0: Mon Apr 24 23:00:57 PDT 2023; root:xnu-7195.141.49.701.3~1/RELEASE_X86_64 x86_64
Weird. If a crate can't be loaded as non-proc-macro first, rustc should try again to load it as proc-macro.

@bjorn3 well ... the is_proc_macro is set to false in rustc_metadata::locator and rejection happens in crate_matched() ... I don't understand how it's supposed to work but I'm kind of a noob there :-)
That is the default value. load_proc_macro sets it to true. maybe_resolve_crate and thus resolve_crate will first call load and then if it fails call load_proc_macro.

@bjorn3 ok, well of course on macOS the locator is looking for .dylib suffix, I just had to rename libmacros.so to libmacros.dylib and now I get further. Need to fix expected u64, found usize error now :-) Thanks again.
... and for the u64 vs. usize error --size_t_is-usize option added to bindgen does the trick it seems (see this issue). Builds ok now!
I think the .dylib issue was seen back then by @Finn Behrens. He managed (I think) to build the kernel in macOS, a year or so ago.
Yes, just found the PR and the blog post, I'll give it a look.
It’s a bit ago that I successfully built it. There is a commit for the c part that I usually apply, and I only ever build on aarch64-Darwin

@Miguel Ojeda @Finn Behrens I've been playing with building the kernel on macOS with the existing CI infra, see https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/pull/824#issuecomment-1641101431. Comments welcome!

sorry, not had time for macos in ages (in part because of lacking a nix devshell for it).
currently failing on elf.h missing. where do you get that file from?
Ah those, gonna repackage them for my nix stuff then, think that makes most sense
